title: Environment Considerations for Microsoft SQL Server
breadcrumbs: Environment Considerations for Microsoft SQL Server
text: Environment Considerations for Microsoft SQL Server The MSS connector makes use of ODBC to connect to Microsoft SQL Server, and requires the correct the ODBC Driver for SQL Server or the SQL Server Native Client version to be installed on the client machine. For versions of the Microsoft SQL Server supported by Uniface, see the Platform Availability Matrix . For the corresponding version of the Microsoft ODBC Driver, consult the Microsoft documentation ( https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/jj730315.aspx ). Permissions For each database you access, you must have select permission on certain system tables. However, database owners can control the permissions on the system tables in their own databases. Table 1. for Which Users Must Have select Permission Table Purpose sysusers Used to check the existence of stored procedures for a specific user. sysobjects Used to check the existence of stored procedures. Network Considerations Microsoft SQL Server always uses a network for accessing the server, even if the server is on the local machine. Accessing servers on other machines is completely transparent for Uniface. For more information on server administration, see the Microsoft SQL Server documentation. The Uniface Server is supported. This also provides transparent network connectivity. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
